review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Coal India Junior Level Executive Salary
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Since I'm serving consultancy subsidiary of Coal India named CMPDI posted at Ranchi (only 5% of total executives gets posting) , it's a sort of white collar job with 2 days weekly off. However, job nature is very widely exposed covering all the facets of mining industry from planning, processing to execution stage which gives a very minute as well as detailed knowledge to carry out a project into operations. Basically deals with Conceptual Reports, Project Feasibilty Report, Coal Gasification, Contract Management, Mine Planning, NIT preparation on various modes, Evaluation of Tenders etc CMPDIL work culture is excellent. Coal India's salary is compatible when compared to other Maharatnas. Those who are posted in field in other subsidiaries gets a very vast exposure of managing human resources as well as gets experience of process as well as maintenance of open cast or undercast mine/Washery/CHP operations which can be very much beneficial to lead the team in future as well as provides great experience to those looking for parallel sector switching jobs.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Work nature is a bit difficult as well as challenging in fields due to remote location postings esp in MCL, SECL & NCL subsidiaries. There the main concern is to meet out the daily/weekly/monthly production targets by overcoming the day to day maintenance & inventory challenges and maintaing IR issues. Dust exposure can't be avoided, certain issues with the quarters of the field, some places have lack of good connectivity as railway station is a bit distant from the work place.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: My experience in my employer company
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: You are not constrained by tough time limit so frequently. There is enough scope of learning from peers. Being a publuc sector enterprise under Govt of India you need not worry about job security. Timely and satisfactory salary payment is another important attraction.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Being a mining company, other allied departmental employees do not get that much importance as the mining department employees do. One should be ready to work in coalfields with unsatisfactory and unhygienic condition under heavy air pollution due to coal and other dusts. Mostly, you have to be ready to serve in remote places except if posted in cities where the company headquarters and sales liaisoning offices are present. You may have to deal with illiterate or less literate mining workers. Being a mining industry, my company has to face frequent shifting of mines which requires land occupation from government, forest  and even residential areas. This results in dealing with the agrieved mass population who may create disturbance with strikes and stalling the company's working.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Certainly not the best but with huge future potential
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
I joined CIL through campus recruitment in 2015 and completing 1 year by october 2016. Presently working in Gevra Open cast project (ASIA'S LARGEST OPEN CAST COAL MINING PROJECT)under SECL, the most profit making subsidiary of CIL.so i am sharing my experience friends. 
1>  Fat pay with minimal input.
2> Unlimited medical benefit to your family members( up to 5 person)
3> LTC,Mobile and PC allowance (once in 3 years), One of the best post retirement plan, pension facility, 3 BHK Quarters from the beginning of your career as an assistant manager, E3 grade (well furnished residential areas are available in selected areas) Affordable housing and car loans etc.
4> Good work life balance. If  your spouse is not a working personnel, you can spend 2-3 hours with her (him) and kids everyday.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Before blaming CIL, let me give you a brief of history of it. CIL  was formed fortnightly by merging some 400+ odd state owned captive mines in 1975 to cater energy demand of  2nd mostly populated country in the globe. Govt. of India had command on its 100% share from 1975-2010 and  attempted a little to change any basic feature. A lot of development has been done after that i.e from 2010 onwards. presently only few  projects have significant infrastructures.My perception regarding Cons is, it reflects the odd features of CIL and we all have to collectively tackle it rather than blaming the management. 
1> Remote Location!!!! can't help you here. As per DGMS guidelines, Mining activity must not be carried  out nearer to any heavily populated area. Still one can find a well developed city in a radius of 30KMs from any project
2> Hazardous Environment.CIL provides  enough safety measures to all its executives and non-executives. Its upto you whether you are using those accessories or not. (I use it and my seniors make mockery of mine.)
3>Poor work culture. That is why CIL is recruiting heavily from top notch institutes of india. It is you who only can change the work culture, stop crying and be the change. I myself being a management trainee stopped the corrupt practice of giving attendance to the workers and getting some favour from them(Money Matters  a lot to we indians). I simply charge sheeted the concerned Sub Ordinate Engineer (he is retiring on 2024), took the topic to Dy. GM level and talked to the workers. Now it am able to completely phase out this evil practice just in 8 months of y joining.
4>Career Growth. In a young executive meeting, I mooted a question before chairman of CIL Mr. Sutirtha Bhattacharya regarding non homogeneous career  path of MINING and Non-MINING branches. His reply was we are bringing a change in it and planning to give an equal career path to the hard working executives upto E7 grade (Up to Dy.GM or AGM post).
5> OBSOLETE working style, doing a little to upgrade latest mining technology available in market.
